zh.lifeSummary: 當William le Boteler於1228年出生在英國藍卡爾沃林頓時，他的父親Alberic Pincerna le Boteler33歲，母親Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington28歲。他於1250年與Dionysia Lostock在英國藍卡爾沃林頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1303年在蘇格蘭度福利爾度福利斯享年75歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾沃林頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William le Boteler于1228年出生在英国藍卡爾沃林頓时，他的父亲Alberic Pincerna le Boteler33岁，母亲Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington28岁。他于1250年与Dionysia Lostock在英国藍卡爾沃林頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1303年在苏格兰度福利爾度福利斯享年75岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾沃林頓。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When William le Boteler was born in 1228, in Warrington, Lancashire, England, his father, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, was 33 and his mother, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, was 28. He married Dionysia Lostock in 1250, in Warrington, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1303, in Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, at the age of 75, and was buried in Warrington, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William le Boteler est né en 1228, à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Dionysia Lostock en 1250, à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1303, à Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Warrington, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William le Boteler 1228 in Warrington, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alberic Pincerna le Boteler 33 und seine Mutter Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington 28. Er hat 1250 in Warrington, Lancashire, England Dionysia Lostock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb 1303 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Scotland und wurde in Warrington, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William le Boteler è nato il nel 1228 nel Warrington, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Dionysia Lostock nel 1250, nel Warrington, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1303, nel Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Warrington, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William le Botelerが1228年にイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンで生まれた時，父Alberic Pincerna le Botelerは33歳，母Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warringtonは28歳であった。1250年にイギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンでDionysia Lostockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1303年にスコットランドダムフリーシュアイアダムフリーズで75歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアワーリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William le Boteler(이)가 1228년 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alberic Pincerna le Boteler(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1250년 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에서 Dionysia Lostock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1303년 스컷랜드 드프리실르 드프리스에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 워링턴에 묻혔다.

mn.Bewsey Hall, Bewsey, Warrington, Cheshir: Bosley, Legeceasterscir, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William le Boteler nasceu em 1228, em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Dionysia Lostock em 1250, em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1303, em Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William le Boteler родился в 1228 г. в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alberic Pincerna le Boteler было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Dionysia Lostock в 1250 г. в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1303 г. в местности думфриесь, думфриессхир, Шотландия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности варрингтон, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William le Boteler nació en 1228, en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Alberic Pincerna le Boteler, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Lady Alicia Garnet Baroness of Warrington, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Dionysia Lostock en 1250, en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1303, en Dumfries, Dumfriesshire, Scotland, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Warrington, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
